name: Monstera obliqua 'Sangay Form'
aboutText: The Monstera obliqua 'Sangay Form' is a highly sought-after, delicate climber originating from the montane rainforests of the Sangay region in Ecuador. Unlike more heavily perforated forms, this selection is prized for its refined, narrow, oblong-lanceolate leaves that remain largely intact even as the plant matures. It thrives in cool, humid environments with filtered light, naturally ascending tree trunks via fine aerial roots. This specific locality form represents a distinct, stable expression of the species that appeals to serious collectors of rare Aroid genetics.

fieldNotes.title: Sangay National Park Material
fieldNotes.author: Aroid Aaron
fieldNotes.content: A locality form of Monstera obliqua associated with the Sangay region of Ecuador — the area around Sangay National Park, a UNESCO World Heritage Site containing active volcanoes and extraordinary biodiversity at multiple altitudinal zones. Ultra rare at top tier pricing. Material from protected area localities carries a particular significance: it represents plant diversity that has survived within conservation boundaries, and whatever found its way into cultivation was collected before restrictions tightened, or through legitimate research channels. Worth knowing the provenance of.
